epigraph.text: If you don't read the newspaper, you're uninformed; if you do read the newspaper, you're misinformed.

follyOfTheDay.headline: Ivy League spends $47K to advocate for rocks' rights
follyOfTheDay.summary: In a stunning testament to the entropy consuming American higher education, an Ivy League institution has allocated nearly fifty thousand dollars annually for a full-time administrator tasked with promoting geological diversity and inclusion—a position bearing the Orwellian title 'DEI Coordinator for Rocks.' The university's commitment to ensuring that sedimentary formations are not marginalized demonstrates a bureaucratic acumen so breathtakingly divorced from reality that even Kafka would blush. One might inquire whether the institution's geology department had been abolished or if its faculty deemed themselves incapable of discussing rocks without institutional mediation. The coordinator's mandate remains deliberately vague, suggesting the university itself is uncertain what the role entails—a fitting metaphor for contemporary academia's relationship with purpose itself.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: At last, rocks finally have representation in the halls of power.
